questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: In the shown figure, both the plates are moving. The fluid between the plates moves such that fluid particles in contact of the plates move with same velocity as the plates. The fluid particles in the gap moves in a linear velocity distribution function. As per Newton law of viscosity, the tangential force F is given by $F=\eta \mathrm{A} \frac{\mathrm{u}-\mathrm{u}_0}{\mathrm{y}}$ Where $\eta=$ coefficient of viscosity $=10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{sec} / \mathrm{m}^3$, $A=$ Area of the plate $=1 \mathrm{~m}^2, \mathrm{y}=$ distance between both the plates $=2 \mathrm{~cm}$. The velocity gradient is given by $\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dy}}$.
Question: In the previous question, the constant velocity of the upper plate would be :
questions.solution:
The velocity of upper plate is same as that of the layer in contact with the plate i.e. layer at $y=2$ cm .
$u=100(0.02)+1=3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A tube of length $\lambda$ and radius R carries a steady flow of a fluid whose density is $\rho$ and viscosity $\eta$. The velocity v of flow is given by $\mathrm{v}=\mathrm{v}_0\left[1-\left(\mathrm{r}^2 / R^2\right)\right]$, where r is the distance of flowing fluid from the axis. ThenQuestion: The difference of pressure at the end of the tube is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & P(2 \pi r) d r=2 \pi r \ell \eta \frac{d v}{d r}-2 \pi(r+d r) \ell \eta \frac{d v}{d r} \\ & P(2 \pi r) d r=-2 \pi \ell \eta\left(\frac{d v}{d r}\right) d r \\ & P(2 \pi r)=-2 \pi \ell \eta\left(\frac{-2 v_0 r}{R^2}\right) \\ & P=\frac{2 \eta \ell v_0}{R^2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A container contains three liquid as shown in the figure. The specific gravity of liquid I and liquid II are 0.8 and 1.2 respectively. The is small hole having area $10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^2$ at point $P$ as shown in the figure. Initially hole is closed. The cross-sectional area of container is $50 \mathrm{~cm}^2$. Assume all liquids are ideal and neglect any type of friction between liquid and container. Read the passage carefully and answer the following question(s) (given that $\mathrm{g}=9.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ and $\mathrm{h}=28 \mathrm{~cm}$ )
Question:The value of $h_2 / h_1$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & P_0+0.8 \rho_w g h+\rho_w g(2 h)+1.2 \rho_w g(3 h)=P_0+1.2 \rho_w g h_2 \\ & \Rightarrow 1.2 h_2=h[0.8+2+3.6]=6.4 h \Rightarrow h_2=\frac{6.4}{1.2} h \\ & P_0+0.8 \rho_w g h+\rho_w g(2 h)=\rho_w g h_1+P_0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}_1=\mathrm{h}[0.8+2] \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}_1=2.8 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \mathrm{~h}_2 / \mathrm{h}_1=40 / 21\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: In the shown figure, both the plates are moving. The fluid between the plates moves such that fluid particles in contact of the plates move with same velocity as the plates. The fluid particles in the gap moves in a linear velocity distribution function. As per Newton law of viscosity, the tangential force F is given by $F=\eta \mathrm{A} \frac{\mathrm{u}-\mathrm{u}_0}{\mathrm{y}}$ Where $\eta=$ coefficient of viscosity $=10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{sec} / \mathrm{m}^3$, $A=$ Area of the plate $=1 \mathrm{~m}^2, \mathrm{y}=$ distance between both the plates $=2 \mathrm{~cm}$. The velocity gradient is given by $\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dy}}$.
Question: If the tangential force $F=1$ Newton and $\mathrm{u}_0=1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, then the velocity profile for the given figure is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & F=\eta A \frac{u-u_0}{y} \\ & \Rightarrow u=\frac{F}{\eta A} y+u_0 \\ & \Rightarrow u=\frac{1}{10^{-2} \times 1} y+1=100 y+1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A container contains three liquid as shown in the figure. The specific gravity of liquid I and liquid II are 0.8 and 1.2 respectively. The is small hole having area $10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^2$ at point $P$ as shown in the figure. Initially hole is closed. The cross-sectional area of container is $50 \mathrm{~cm}^2$. Assume all liquids are ideal and neglect any type of friction between liquid and container. Read the passage carefully and answer the following question(s) (given that $\mathrm{g}=9.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ and $\mathrm{h}=28 \mathrm{~cm}$ )
Question: At some instant, hole is open, the speed of water coming out from hole just after the opening of hole is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & P_0+\frac{1}{2} \rho_w v^2=P_0+0.8 \rho_w g h+\rho_w g(2 h) \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{v^2}{2}=g h[0.8+2] \\ & v=\sqrt{2 \times 9.8 \times 0.28 \times 2.8}=3.92 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A smooth and vertical circular wire frame of radius 2 m is fixed inside water as shown. A small bead of specific gravity 0.5 is threaded on the wire and is kept at the origin. If the bead is imparted velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ towards positive $x$ axis, it moves on the wire frame then neglecting effect of viscosity
Question: Inside water, as bead moves up, with time its speed.
questions.solution: Inside water $\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{t}}=\mathrm{g} \cos \theta$ as it moves up, with time $\mathrm{t}, \theta$ decreases $\therefore a_t$ increases i.e. speed increases and rate of increase of speed $\left(a_t\right)$ also increases
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:

Question: If bead is given minimum velocity $\mathrm{V}_0$ at origin as calculated in first question then acceleration 'a' of bead just after it comes out of water is:
